definition_population.content: Chronic inflammatory arthritis in a subset of psoriasis patients; spondyloarthritis family; involves peripheral joints, entheses, spine, skin/nails.

epidemiology.content: Not established in this pass — no quotable numeric prevalence/incidence figure was retrieved; broad literature exists (3,325 PubMed hits on the general query) but a specific verbatim number was not pulled through.

biology.content: PsA pathogenesis centers on IL-23/IL-17 axis. KIF16B is a kinesin-3 family motor protein mediating PI(3)P-dependent endosomal cargo transport; no direct literature ties KIF16B to PsA or T-cell immune biology — link is inferred from platform DE ranking and OT genetic score only.

sources.quote: This article reviews the immunologic role of interleukin (IL)-17, the major effector cytokine in the pathogenesis of psoriatic disease
kinesin family member 16B (KIF16B) has a characteristic PhoX homology (PX) domain that binds to membranes containing phosphatidylinositol-3-phosphate (PI(3)P)
KIF16B is a newly identified kinesin that regulates the intracellular motility of early endosomes.

treatment.content: IL-17/IL-23 biologics and JAK inhibitors (incl. TYK2 inhibitor deucravacitinib, FDA NDA214958/SOTYKTU) are used in psoriatic disease; JAK class carries an FDA black-box safety warning. No PsA-specific FDA approval for deucravacitinib confirmed in this pass. No approved therapy targets KIF16B.

sources.quote: The US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) recently placed a black box warning on this class of medications due to safety concerns based on data from studies investigating tofacitinib
Successful Management of Multidrug-Resistant Psoriatic Arthritis Coexisting With Rheumatoid Arthritis Using a TYK2 Inhibitor Deucravacitinib-Based Regimen

regulatory_trials.content: 372 total interventional trials for psoriatic arthritis on ClinicalTrials.gov. Notable Phase 2 trial: NCT05715125 (VTX958), terminated.

sources.quote: A Phase 2, Multicenter, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Parallel-Group Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of VTX958 in Patients With Active Psoriatic Arthritis

gaps: No direct PubMed evidence linking KIF16B to psoriatic arthritis or T-cell biology (0 hits on direct search).
No quotable numeric epidemiology figure retrieved.
PsA-specific FDA approval status for deucravacitinib not confirmed.
